round1: Round 1 is done, and if your bracket looks like a dumpster fire, you're in good company; almost no one had Vegas sweeping the Kings. Shoutout to Jake for actually seeing the Sharks' broom, and a collective 'bless your heart' to everyone who picked the Avs, Wild, or Blue Jackets to win.
round2: Round 2 is in the books, and while the hockey was good, most of your picks suggest you were watching a different sport entirely. A solid majority of you thought Pittsburgh would pull it off, which is just precious, and Jaclyn, Kiersten, and Stephanie somehow managed to miss three out of four series completely. Maybe stick to flipping coins next round.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, and while a few of you actually predicted Washington's seven-game grind, Glenda was the lone wolf who saw Vegas's swift five-game dispatch. For the rest, especially Jamie, Nathan, and Robin, perhaps next year you'll consider picks that don't look like they were pulled from a hat full of wishful thinking.
round4: Washington closed out Vegas in five games, making fools of the nine people who thought the Golden Knights had a prayer. Derrick and Jaclyn were the closest, picking Washington in six, while Nathan and Robin somehow managed to predict a Vegas sweep. Glenda, you picked the right number of games, just for the wrong team; a truly unique way to be wrong.
overall: Alright, another playoff season in the books, and after what felt like an eternity, the Washington Capitals finally stumbled their way to a Stanley Cup. In our little pool, Ryan absolutely dominated with 59 points, proving that picking 11 teams and getting 8 game counts correct is apparently the secret formula to victory, leaving Glenda a distant second. Derrick and Marc, bless their hearts, managed to pick 14 teams each, only to barely crack 40 points – a true masterclass in quantity over quality, folks. Meanwhile, our very own Robin, with a truly astounding 12 points and only 4 teams picked, clearly demonstrated that some people just aren't meant to be involved in sports, or perhaps even pick lottery numbers. Better luck next year, everyone, especially those who apparently still don't understand how hockey works.
